message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "single rune yields 25G base premium"
Expected: quote with one rune returns { premium: 40 } (25 base + 10 first insurance + 5 fee)
Current error: Expected { premium: 40 }, received { premium: NaN }
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.

The test expects `quote({ items: [{ type: "rune" }], ... })` to return `{ premium: 40 }`. The breakdown: 25G base + 10G first insurance + 5G fee = 40G.

Currently `BASE_PREMIUMS["rune"]` is undefined, causing NaN. The minimal fix is to add `rune: 25` to the BASE_PREMIUMS map.

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_03-35-32_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts has been updated successfully.

content.text: Now let's verify the test passes.

content.text:
All tests pass (6 passed, 43 todo).

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added `rune: 25` to the BASE_PREMIUMS map.
**Result**: All tests now pass (6 passing, 43 todo)
**Approach**: This is minimal because the existing `premiumForItem` function already handles base premium + first insurance surcharge, and `quote` already adds the processing fee. The only missing piece was the rune's base premium value (25G), so adding a single entry to the lookup map was the smallest possible change to make the test pass. No new logic was introduced.
